examples.title: Linear Recursion - Factorial
examples.description: Demonstrates linear recursion using factorial calculation
examples.explanation: Each call multiplies n by the factorial of (n-1), creating a linear chain of calls until reaching the base case n=1.

examples.title: Binary Recursion - Fibonacci
examples.description: Demonstrates binary recursion using Fibonacci calculation (naive version)
examples.explanation: Each call makes two recursive calls to compute the two previous Fibonacci numbers, forming a binary tree of calls.

examples.title: Binary Recursion - Binary Search
examples.description: Demonstrates linear recursion pattern in binary search
examples.explanation: Although binary search divides problem into halves, it makes only one recursive call per iteration (linear recursion pattern).

examples.title: Tree Recursion - Tower of Hanoi
examples.description: Demonstrates tree recursion with Tower of Hanoi puzzle
examples.code:
def tower_of_hanoi(n, source, target, auxiliary):
    # Base case: only one disk
    if n == 1:
        print(f"Move disk 1 from {source} to {target}")
        return
    
    # Recursive case: three steps
    # 1. Move n-1 disks from source to auxiliary
    tower_of_hanoi(n - 1, source, auxiliary, target)
    
    # 2. Move largest disk from source to target
    print(f"Move disk {n} from {source} to {target}")
    
    # 3. Move n-1 disks from auxiliary to target
    tower_of_hanoi(n - 1, auxiliary, target, source)

# Example: tower_of_hanoi(3, 'A', 'C', 'B')
examples.explanation: Makes more than two recursive calls per level, creating a tree-like branching structure. Total moves = 2^n - 1.
